mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate at or near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) signals maximum monetary accommodation. Under this regime, equity valuations expand due to compressed discount rates and reduced opportunity cost of equity capital relative to cash. This produces positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window during zero-rate regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals strengthening labor demand and income growth, which increases consumer purchasing power and corporate earnings expectations. This mechanism operates continuously as a trend signal: higher month-over-month PAYEMS growth predicts positive equity returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS 3-month growth rate and 60-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or correlation reverses sign persistently.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer and residential demand strength. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, this indicates broad-based demand expansion that supports corporate profit growth. The joint signal predicts positive 20-day SPX returns more reliably than either series alone.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both show positive 1-month growth
failure_condition: Rank IC of the joint RSAFS+HOUST growth signal falls below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or the interaction effect (measured as the coefficient on the product term) is not statistically significant at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining month-over-month signals supply-side weakness and potential demand destruction. Under supply-constrained regimes, falling INDPRO predicts negative equity returns as it reflects deteriorating manufacturing activity and reduced corporate capacity utilization, which compresses margins over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances when INDPRO declines > 0.5% MoM, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) rising above 3% year-over-year combined with FEDFUNDS below 2% creates a negative real interest rate regime. This regime erodes purchasing power and signals monetary policy lag relative to inflation, producing negative equity returns as investors demand higher risk premiums. The mechanism operates over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 65% of instances when negative real rates are present, or the interaction term coefficient is not significant at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer confidence. This triggers a negative equity signal as forward earnings expectations decline due to lower consumer spending and potential recession risk. The mechanism operates over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 58% of instances when UNRATE rises > 0.3pp over 3 months, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured as quarter-over-quarter annualized rate above 2.5% signals robust economic expansion. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as higher growth supports corporate revenue and earnings expansion across the business cycle.
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth and 60-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or the correlation is negative and statistically significant.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) both declining simultaneously signal broad-based economic contraction affecting both supply capacity and labor demand. This joint deterioration predicts negative 5-day SPX returns as it indicates imminent earnings downgrades and recession risk. The interaction effect is stronger than either series alone.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 0.3% MoM AND PAYEMS declines > 0.1% MoM
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 62% of instances when both INDPRO and PAYEMS decline simultaneously, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant at p < 0.10.
